statement.en: Modulating Cross-Modal Convergence with Single-Stimulus, Intra-Modal Dispersion
statement.summary: Neural networks exhibit a remarkable degree of representational convergence across diverse architectures, training objectives, and even data modalities. This convergence is predictive of alignment with brain representation. A recent hypothesis suggests this arises from learning the underlying structure in the environment in similar ways. However, it is unclear how individual stimuli elicit convergent representations across networks. An image can be perceived in multiple ways and expressed differently using words. Here, we introduce a methodology based on the Generalized Procrustes Algorithm to measure intra-modal representational convergence at the single-stimulus level. We applied this to vision models with distinct training objectives, selecting stimuli based on their degree of alignment

proposer: Eghbal A. Hosseini, Brian Cheung, Evelina Fedorenko et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The paper presents a novel methodological framework (Generalized Procrustes Algorithm applied to single-stimulus intra-modal convergence) that is empirically testable but not yet peer-reviewed in final form (dated 2026-04-25, appears preprint). The core claim—that representational convergence can be modulated and measured at single-stimulus granularity—is theoretically sound but requires validation across datasets and architectures. Status: provisional, under validation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 個々の刺激は、内部分散の大きさによってネットワーク間の収束を制御する隠れた変数のようにふるまう。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 複数の感覚器官が一つの刺激を異なる角度から「見つめ」るほど、その内部の分散が増すことで逆説的に整列が深まる。
